architectureVision.statement: Enable eMobility through an integrated enterprise architecture spanning business, application, data, and technology domains.
drivers: Reduce fragmentation across organizational and technology change.
Make dependencies and ownership explicit across architecture layers.
Improve governance of strategic change and target-state decisions.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Urban EV Driver outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Session Success Rate for Urban EV Driver by advancing reliability and transparency through accurate real-time data and billing clarity.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Corporate Fleet Manager (EV Transition) outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Average Cost per kWh for Corporate Fleet Manager (EV Transition) by advancing cost transparency and reimbursement through unified billing and reporting.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Urban EV Driver outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Session Success Rate for Urban EV Driver by advancing reliability and transparency through accurate real-time data and billing clarity.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Corporate Fleet Manager (EV Transition) outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Average Cost per kWh for Corporate Fleet Manager (EV Transition) by advancing cost transparency and reimbursement through unified billing and reporting.

principles: Business capabilities drive application and technology design.
Information is modeled as a shared enterprise asset.
Technology choices should be standardized unless a domain-specific need justifies variation.
Implementation should be governed against explicit target and transition architectures.

stakeholders.name: Enterprise CPO Operations Director
stakeholders.concern: Senior leader at a large European Charge Point Operator responsible for operating and scaling thousands of public and semi-public EV charge points. Accountable for uptime, regulatory compliance, roaming interoperability, and long-term profitability of the charging network.

stakeholders.name: Head of Product at eMSP
stakeholders.concern: Product leader at a European mobility service provider offering EV drivers access to charging via apps and charge cards. Responsible for driver experience, roaming coverage, and transaction success across multiple CPO networks.

stakeholders.name: Urban EV Driver
stakeholders.concern: Private EV owner living in a European city who relies on a mix of public, workplace, and occasional highway charging. Values convenience, price transparency, and reliable charging access across borders.

stakeholders.name: Corporate Fleet Manager (EV Transition)
stakeholders.concern: Fleet manager at a mid-to-large enterprise transitioning from ICE to electric vehicles. Responsible for total cost of ownership, driver reimbursement, charging policy enforcement, and ESG reporting.
